label: Audit
hypothesisStem: If the current weakness in this dimension is described precisely, later rounds can target the real failure instead of a surface symptom.
mutationMoves: Name the current failure mode in one sentence referencing specific lines/sections in target files
List the rules, patterns, or gaps causing the failure
Quantify the gap: how many cases does the current system handle vs how many it should
Translate the weakness into a concrete quality metric degradation
acceptanceAdditions: The weakness is framed as a falsifiable claim
The audit references specific section headers in target files
deliverables: A named failure mode with evidence from target files
A gap quantification table (have vs need)

label: Research
hypothesisStem: If authoritative sources are identified for this dimension, the formalization in slot 3 will have solid theoretical grounding.
mutationMoves: Identify 3-5 authoritative sources (academic papers, practitioner books, film analysis)
Extract the key finding or framework from each source in 2-3 sentences
Map each finding to a specific rule or pattern that could be added
Note contradictions between sources and resolve with a principled position
acceptanceAdditions: Each source has a citation and one-sentence relevance statement
At least one source is from cognitive science or perception research
deliverables: Annotated source list with extracted findings
A source-to-rule mapping table

label: Formalize
hypothesisStem: If the research findings are converted to precise rules, LLMs can execute them without ambiguity.
mutationMoves: Convert each research finding into an IF/THEN decision rule
Define the rule's scope (when it applies and when it does not)
Write the rule in the existing format/notation of the target file
Add a counter-example showing what violation of the rule looks like
acceptanceAdditions: Every new rule has predicate form, scope, and counter-example
Rules do not contradict existing rules or contradictions are resolved
deliverables: New rules written in target file format
A rule consistency check against existing rules

label: Expand
hypothesisStem: If the new rules are expanded with examples, variants, and edge cases, the methodology becomes robust against real-world complexity.
mutationMoves: Add 2-3 worked examples per new rule showing correct application
Document variant forms of each rule (strong form vs weak form)
Identify edge cases where the rule breaks down and add exception handling
Cross-link to related rules in other reference files
acceptanceAdditions: Each rule has at least 2 worked examples
Edge cases documented with resolution strategy
deliverables: Expanded rule entries with examples, variants, edge cases
Cross-reference index to related rules

label: Apply
hypothesisStem: If the new rules produce better results on Mirror Visitor ch1 than the previous version, the formalization is validated.
mutationMoves: Apply the new/modified rules to Mirror Visitor ch1 scene
Generate a storyboard fragment using the updated methodology
Compare the output against the previous version on quality metrics
Note where the new rules improved the output and where they made no difference
acceptanceAdditions: The test storyboard covers at least 7 shots
At least one quality metric improved or baseline established
deliverables: A test storyboard fragment for Mirror Visitor ch1
A before/after metric comparison table

label: Refine
hypothesisStem: If the rules are adjusted based on application results, false positives and false negatives decrease.
mutationMoves: Analyze test results for false positives and false negatives
Adjust rule thresholds, scope boundaries, or exception lists
Re-run the failing cases to confirm the fix
Update the rule documentation with the adjustment rationale
acceptanceAdditions: False positive rate on the test scene < 20%
Adjustments are documented with before/after comparison
deliverables: Refined rule set with adjustment rationale
Updated metric targets if the original targets were unrealistic

label: Integrate
hypothesisStem: If the new rules connect cleanly to adjacent dimensions, the methodology avoids internal contradictions and coverage gaps.
mutationMoves: Trace how the new rules affect adjacent dimensions
Update cross-references in affected files
Check for rule conflicts between dimensions and resolve them
Add integration notes to the QUICKREF.md
acceptanceAdditions: No unresolved cross-dimension conflicts
QUICKREF.md reflects new additions
deliverables: Updated cross-references in all affected files
Integration conflict resolution log

label: Evaluate
hypothesisStem: If the accumulated changes produce measurable improvement on quality metrics, the band objective is being achieved.
mutationMoves: Run the full evaluation on the test storyboard using refined rules
Compare scores against the band's baseline
Identify which metrics improved, stayed flat, or regressed
Compute the overall band improvement delta
acceptanceAdditions: At least 3 of 6 metrics improved or none regressed
Overall weighted score improved by at least 2 points
deliverables: Full evaluation report with metric comparison table
Band progress assessment (on-track / at-risk / blocked)

label: Stress-test
hypothesisStem: If the methodology survives adversarial attack, it is robust enough for production use.
mutationMoves: Design 2-3 adversarial test cases that should break the new rules
Run the methodology on these adversarial cases
Classify breakages as acceptable edge cases, fixable bugs, or fundamental limitations
Fix fixable bugs and document limitations
acceptanceAdditions: At least 2 adversarial test cases attempted
No undocumented fundamental limitations
deliverables: Adversarial test case descriptions and results
Bug fix list and limitation catalog

label: Codify
hypothesisStem: If the band's gains are written into canonical reference files and the evomap is updated, the improvement is permanent and visible to future rounds.
mutationMoves: Write all surviving rules, patterns, and examples into canonical target files
Update SKILL.md with any new commands, parameters, or workflow changes
Update QUICKREF.md with the condensed versions
Update sb-evomap.md with the version increment and change log
acceptanceAdditions: All target files have no broken cross-references
sb-evomap.md version incremented
The band's acceptance checks all pass
deliverables: Updated reference files with final edits
Updated QUICKREF.md
Updated sb-evomap.md with version entry
Band completion checkpoint note
